title: Start Here
summary: Landing page for humans and LLMs.
Explains the public pages, source files, internal claim markers, the dossier's source posture, and the method for reading scandals through system-level political analysis.

title: Political context
summary: Sets the political frame without the informal shorthand of the earlier build.
Restores the dossier's direct warning that legacy-media and platform consensus are not neutral referees.

title: System logic: narrative inversion, dependency, and entrenchment
summary: Uses the agenda paper as the conceptual backbone and restores its strongest concrete points.

Frames the broader pattern as a sequence with institutional receipts, not as a pile of disconnected disputes, and explains how aligned behavior can arise through direct coordination, factional reinforcement, or incentive convergence.

title: Elite factions inside one shared system
summary: Restores the dossier's direct factional framing: Davos-ESG-DEI versus America First and resource-capital.
Explains why ownership, media, and policy alignment do not require a single all-powerful cabal.

title: Ownership layer: Big Three and legacy media
summary: Restores the dossier's direct ownership framing while keeping the distinction between ownership influence and day-to-day editorial control.
Names the major media parents and the governance layer that sits above them.

title: The censorship and compliance network beyond ownership
summary: Restores the named censorship stack in full: TNI, EIP, DFRLab, IFCN, Poynter, PolitiFact, GARM, regulators, and platform policy.
Adds the Poynter/IFCN funding and platform-enforcement overlap while avoiding the weaker claim that donors directly command individual ratings.

title: Manufactured outrage and narrative laundering
summary: Turns the manufactured-outrage paper into a full case-study section instead of a short nod.

Shows how useful narratives are amplified fast, then corrected quietly after the damage is done, and explains why scandal coverage can also function as diversion from upstream institutional pressure points.

title: Protected categories, compelled language, and race-explicit preferences
summary: Expands the trans and white-discrimination receipts instead of compressing them into a light overview.
Keeps the point narrow and concrete: real institutional enforcement existed, and courts often had to step in.

title: The money layer: NGOs, dark-money surfaces, and managed activism
summary: Upgrades the NGO section into a fuller map of funded activism, bail support, and propaganda-adjacent surfaces.
Connects the spending layer to the narrative-control layer instead of treating them as separate worlds.

title: Immigration, demographic clientelism, and Canada as case study
summary: Uses the Canada intake paper as a full case study instead of a light example.
Restores the dossier's direct claim that permissive growth came first and visible damage control came later.

title: Trade leverage, tariffs, and Canada's weak hand
summary: Keeps the tariff paper proportionate while restoring the strongest claims and evidence.
Frames tariffs as one of the clearest external pressure tools used by the second-term Trump administration.

title: What the second-term Trump administration is actually hitting
summary: Restores the original dossier's pressure-point map and its vivid real-world harm examples.
Keeps the events factual while treating the surrounding narrative climate as part of the dossier's analysis.

title: Why accountability stays narrow and slow
summary: Removes the Bondi-Comey framing and states the constraint logic in general form.
Explains why exposure does not automatically become conviction, and why current visible actors are not always the main upstream cause of incomplete disclosure or stalled accountability.

title: Summary in one pass
summary: One-pass compression of the rebuilt dossier with the sharper framing restored.
Ends with a direct caution against treating legacy media as neutral referees and compresses the dossier's method for reading scandal narratives against upstream system behavior.
